description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from a series of large, rectangular stone blocks, giving it a robust and monumental appearance. The archway itself is a single, wide opening with a gently curved top, framed by thick vertical piers on either side. The surface texture of the stones is rough and uneven, suggesting weathered masonry typical of historical stonework. The structure is relatively simple, lacking any visible decorative reliefs, sculptures, or inscription panels. Both the front and back faces are identical, with no additional ornamentation or architectural embellishments. The overall form is utilitarian and solid, evoking the style of ancient Roman or early medieval triumphal arches.
description: A robust stone triumphal arch with a single wide opening and rough, blocky masonry. The structure is simple, with no visible decorations or inscriptions.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full archway with its wide, curved opening and blocky stone construction.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the arch's narrow depth and solid, rectangular profile.
description_view_2: The back view mirrors the front, displaying the same wide archway and stone block pattern.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again shows the narrow depth and solid stonework.
